steps.title: Use the angle information
steps.content:
Both triangles have angles measuring $32^{\circ}$ and $46^{\circ}$. The third angle in each triangle is
$$180^{\circ}-32^{\circ}-46^{\circ}=102^{\circ}$$
So the triangles are similar by equal corresponding angles.

steps.title: Need one corresponding side
steps.content: Similar triangles are congruent only if their scale factor is $1$. The sides opposite the $102^{\circ}$ angles are $BC$ and $EF$, so knowing both lengths is enough to decide whether the scale factor is $1$. If those corresponding sides are equal, all corresponding sides are equal. The answer is C.
